mission.title: Install and Configure OSCAL Compass (Compliance Trestle)
mission.description: OSCAL Compass Compliance Trestle is an opinionated tooling platform for managing compliance as code using NIST's OSCAL standard. It provides CI/CD-friendly tools to create, validate, and manage OSCAL documents for compliance automation. Requires Python 3.10+.

steps.title: Create a Python virtual environment
steps.description:
Create and activate an isolated Python environment for compliance-trestle.

```bash
python3 -m venv trestle-env
source trestle-env/bin/activate
```

> Requires Python 3.10 or later.

steps.title: Install compliance-trestle via pip
steps.description:
Install the compliance-trestle package from PyPI.

```bash
pip install compliance-trestle
```

> This installs the trestle CLI and all dependencies.

steps.title: Verify installation
steps.description:
Confirm trestle is installed and check its version.

```bash
trestle version
```

> Should display version 4.0.2 or later.

steps.title: Initialize a trestle workspace
steps.description:
Create a new trestle project workspace for managing OSCAL documents.

```bash
mkdir my-compliance && cd my-compliance
trestle init
```

> This creates the directory structure for catalogs, profiles, component-definitions, and other OSCAL model types.

steps.title: Import an OSCAL catalog
steps.description:
Import the NIST SP 800-53 catalog to get started with a standard set of security controls.

```bash
trestle import -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/usnistgov/oscal-content/main/nist.gov/SP800-53/rev5/json/NIST_SP-800-53_rev5_catalog.json -o nist-800-53-rev5
```

> This imports the catalog into your workspace under the catalogs directory.

uninstall.title: Uninstall compliance-trestle
uninstall.description:
Remove compliance-trestle from your environment.

```bash
pip uninstall compliance-trestle -y
```
